116	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
117	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
118	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are

126	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
127	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
128	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer
129	// directly.
130	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
131		ctx context.Context,
132		projectRoot string,
133		b *bead.Bead,
134		res *ExecuteBeadResult,
135		submit func(LandRequest) (*LandResult, error),
136		eventStore BeadEventAppender,
137		actor, source string,
138		now func() time.Time,
139	) (*LandResult, *PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
140		if now == nil {
141			now = time.Now
142		}
143		if submit == nil {
144			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("submit-with-pre-merge-checks: submit callback required")
145		}
146		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir)
147		outcome, err := RunPreMergeChecks(ctx, projectRoot, b, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, evidenceDir)
148		if err != nil {
149			// Treat checks_bypass / loader errors as a hard preserve: the worker
150			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park
151			// the result under an iteration ref than to silently land it.
152			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
153			if upErr := (&RealOrchestratorGitOps{}).UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
154				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result after checks setup error: %w (original error: %v)", upErr, err)
155			}
156			return &LandResult{
157				Status:      "preserved",
158				PreserveRef: ref,
159				Reason:      "pre-merge checks setup error: " + err.Error(),
160			}, nil, nil
161		}
162		if outcome.Blocked {
163			land, perr := PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks(projectRoot, res, outcome, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{})
164			if perr != nil {
165				return nil, outcome, perr
166			}
167			if eventStore != nil {
168				_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
169			}
170			return land, outcome, nil
171		}
172		if eventStore != nil && len(outcome.Bypassed) > 0 {
173			_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
174		}
175		land, sErr := submit(BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res))
176		return land, outcome, sErr
177	}

488		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
489			writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())

491	
492		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
493			"worker_id":    runtime.WorkerID,
494			"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
495			"harness":      harness,
496			"model":        model,
497			"session_id":   runtime.SessionID,
498			"assignee":     assignee,
499			"once":         runtime.Once,
500		})
501		cleanupLog := runtime.CleanupLog
502		if cleanupLog == nil {
503			cleanupLog = runtime.Log
504		}
505		cleanupStop := startExecutionCleanupWorker(ctx, runtime.ProjectRoot, runtime.CleanupRunner, runtime.CleanupInterval, runtime.CleanupTickCh, cleanupLog, emit)
506		defer cleanupStop()
507		_, _, _ = runExecutionCleanupPass(ctx, runtime.ProjectRoot, runtime.CleanupRunner, cleanupLog, emit, "startup")
508		// exitReason is populated as the loop exits to surface a structured reason
509		// in the loop.end event (ddx-dc157075 AC #4). Work-owned terminal reasons
510		// are classified through work.StopCondition; fatal_config,
511		// preflight_failed, resource_exhausted, and future provider exhaustion are
512		// still subsystem-specific exits.
513		exitReason := ""
514		applyStop := func(input work.StopInput) bool {
515			decision, ok := work.ClassifyStop(input)
516			if !ok {
517				return false
518			}
519			exitReason = decision.ExitReason
520			return true
521		}
522		if runtime.RoutePreflight != nil {
523			if rerr := runtime.RoutePreflight(ctx, harness, model); rerr != nil {
524				detail := fmt.Sprintf("routing preflight rejected (harness=%s model=%s): %s",
525					harness, model, rerr.Error())
526				if runtime.Log != nil {
527					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "routing preflight: %s (startup)\n", detail)
528				}
529				emit("preflight.rejected", map[string]any{
530					"harness": harness,
531					"model":   model,
532					"reason":  rerr.Error(),
533					"startup": true,
534				})
535				report := ExecuteBeadReport{
536					Status:           ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
537					Detail:           detail,
538					Harness:          harness,
539					Model:            model,
540					Disrupted:        true,
541					DisruptionReason: "preflight_rejected",
542					OutcomeReason:    "preflight_failed",
543				}
544				emitDisruptionDetected(emit, w.Store, "", "preflight_rejected", detail, harness, model, assignee, now().UTC())
545				result.Failures++
546				result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
547				result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
548				exitReason = "preflight_failed"
549				return result, nil
550			}
551		}
552		defer func() {
553			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
554				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
555				"successes":           result.Successes,
556				"failures":            result.Failures,
557				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
558				"exit_reason":         exitReason,
559			})
560		}()
561	
562		for {
563			_, _, _ = runExecutionCleanupPass(ctx, runtime.ProjectRoot, runtime.CleanupRunner, cleanupLog, emit, "pre-claim")
564			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
565			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
566			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
567			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
568			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
569				if exitReason == "" {
570					applyStop(work.StopInput{ContextErr: err})
571				}
572				return result, err
573			}
574			if runtime.BudgetStop != nil {
575				if budgetReport, stopped := runtime.BudgetStop(); stopped {
576					applyStop(work.StopInput{Budget: true})
577					if budgetReport.Status == "" {
578						budgetReport.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
579					}
580					if budgetReport.Detail != "" && runtime.Log != nil {
581						_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(runtime.Log, budgetReport.Detail)
582					}
583					result.Failures++
584					result.LastFailureStatus = budgetReport.Status
585					result.Results = append(result.Results, budgetReport)
586					return result, nil
587				}
588			}
589	
590			candidate, skips, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(ctx, result.Results[resultsResetIdx:], []work.Guard{complexityGuard, preclaimGuard}, runtime.LabelFilter, runtime.TargetBeadID)
591			if err != nil {
592				exitReason = "fatal_config"
593				return result, err
594			}
595			// Diagnostic: when nextCandidate selected a lower-priority bead while
596			// at least one higher-priority bead was skipped, emit a structured
597			// picker.priority_skip event naming each skipped bead and the reason
598			// (ddx-9d55601f AC #4). Without this surface, an operator who sees a
599			// worker claim a P2 while P0s sit in `ddx bead ready` cannot tell
600			// whether the picker has a bug, the P0s are filtered upstream, or a
601			// label filter is in play.
602			if ok {
603				emitPickerPrioritySkips(emit, candidate, skips)
604			}
605			if !ok {
606				if hasGuardSkips(skips) {
607					continue
608				}
609				if result.Attempts == 0 {
610					result.NoReadyWork = true
611					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
612						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
613							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
614								SkippedEpics:                 breakdown.SkippedEpics,
615								SkippedEpicClosureCandidates: breakdown.SkippedEpicClosureCandidates,
616								SkippedOnCooldown:            breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
617								SkippedNeedsInvestigation:    breakdown.SkippedNeedsInvestigation,
618								SkippedBlocked:               breakdown.SkippedBlocked,
619								SkippedNotEligible:           breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
620								SkippedSuperseded:            breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
621								NextRetryAfter:               breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
622							}
623						}
624					}
625				}
626				if applyStop(work.StopInput{
627					NoReadyWork:  true,
628					Once:         runtime.Once,
629					PollInterval: runtime.PollInterval,
630				}) {
631					return result, nil
632				}
633				// Long-running drain (poll-interval > 0): an empty queue is idle,
634				// not terminal. --once and explicit poll-zero exits are classified
635				// above through work.StopCondition. Emit a transient "no_ready_work"
636				// event so server-managed workers can surface this as an idle substate
637				// (ddx-dc157075 AC #5) instead of treating it as terminal.
638				emit("loop.idle", map[string]any{
639					"reason":        "no_ready_work",
640					"poll_interval": runtime.PollInterval.String(),
641				})
642				// Surface the idle substate via the progress channel so server-side
643				// drainProgress can flip WorkerRecord.Substate to "idle" without
644				// reading the JSONL event log. Phase="loop.idle" is treated as a
645				// substate signal, not a per-bead attempt phase.
646				emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
647					EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
648					WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
649					ProjectID: runtime.ProjectRoot,
650					Phase:     "loop.idle",
651					Heartbeat: true,
652					TS:        now().UTC(),
653					Message:   "no_ready_work",
654				})
655				resultsResetIdx = len(result.Results)
656				if err := sleepOrWake(ctx, runtime.PollInterval, runtime.WakeCh); err != nil {
657					if exitReason == "" {
658						applyStop(work.StopInput{ContextErr: err})
659					}
660					return result, err
661				}
662				continue
663			}
664	
665			// Found a candidate: clear any "idle" substate set on the previous
666			// no-candidate iteration (ddx-dc157075 AC #5).
667			emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
668				EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
669				WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
670				ProjectID: runtime.ProjectRoot,
671				BeadID:    candidate.ID,
672				Phase:     "loop.active",
673				Heartbeat: true,
674				TS:        now().UTC(),
675			})
676	
677			if allowed, reason := complexityGuard.Allow(ctx, candidate.ID); !allowed {
678				if runtime.Log != nil && reason != "" {
679					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "complexity gate: %s (skipping %s)\n", reason, candidate.ID)
680				}
681				continue
682			}
683			// Claim atomically before model-backed intake and lint to prevent
684			// concurrent workers from issuing duplicate model calls or appending
685			// duplicate lint events for the same bead. Terminal non-actionable
686			// outcomes unclaim and park the bead rather than proceeding to execution.
687			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
688				// Another worker won the race for this bead. Emit a structured
689				// claim_race event so concurrent-worker losses are observable
690				// (ddx-9d55601f AC #4). The bead remains in `attempted` for this
691				// run; on the next iteration it will be filtered out of
692				// ReadyExecution naturally because Claim() flipped it to
693				// in_progress, so the loser keeps moving down priority order.
694				emit("picker.claim_race", map[string]any{
695					"bead_id":    candidate.ID,
696					"priority":   candidate.Priority,
697					"queue_rank": queueRankValue(candidate.Extra["queue-rank"]),
698					"reason":     err.Error(),
699				})
700				continue
701			}
702	
703			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
704				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
705				"title":    candidate.Title,
706				"assignee": assignee,
707			})
708	
709			if runtime.Log != nil {
710				if candidate.Title != "" {
711					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s: %s\n", candidate.ID, candidate.Title)
712				} else {
713					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s\n", candidate.ID)
714				}
715			}
716	
717			// Pre-dispatch intake runs after claiming so that only the owning worker
718			// performs model-backed readiness evaluation. Concurrent workers that lose
719			// the claim race skip intake entirely (picker.claim_race above). Terminal
720			// non-actionable outcomes unclaim and park the bead so it does not
721			// re-appear in ReadyExecution until an operator reviews it.
722			if runtime.PreClaimIntakeHook != nil {
723				if runtime.Log != nil {
724					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "pre-claim intake: starting %s\n", candidate.ID)
725				}
726				emit("pre_claim_intake.start", map[string]any{
727					"bead_id": candidate.ID,
728				})
729				emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
730					EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
731					WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
732					ProjectID: runtime.ProjectRoot,
733					BeadID:    candidate.ID,
734					Phase:     "pre_claim_intake",
735					Heartbeat: true,
736					TS:        now().UTC(),
737					Message:   "pre-claim intake",
738				})
739				intakeResult, intakeErr := runtime.PreClaimIntakeHook(ctx, candidate.ID)
740				intakeOutcome := intakeResult.normalizedOutcome()
741				switch {
742				case intakeErr != nil:
743					warning := trimDiagnosticPrefix(intakeErr.Error(), "pre-claim intake")
744					if runtime.Log != nil {
745						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "pre-claim intake warning: %s (continuing with %s)\n", warning, candidate.ID)
746					}
747					emit("pre_claim_intake.warn", map[string]any{
748						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
749						"outcome": string(PreClaimIntakeError),
750						"detail":  warning,
751					})
752				case intakeOutcome == PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic:
753					// pass-through
754				case intakeOutcome == PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten:

760						emit("pre_claim_intake.blocked", map[string]any{
761							"bead_id": candidate.ID,
762							"outcome": string(PreClaimIntakeAmbiguousNeedsHuman),
763							"detail":  warning,
764						})
765						_ = w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID)
766						if runtime.Once {
767							applyStop(work.StopInput{Once: true})

769						}
770						continue
771					}
772				case intakeOutcome == PreClaimIntakeError:
773					warning := trimDiagnosticPrefix(intakeResult.Detail, "pre-claim intake")
774					if warning == "" {
775						warning = "pre-claim intake returned intake_error"
776					}
777					if runtime.Log != nil {
778						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "pre-claim intake warning: %s (continuing with %s)\n", warning, candidate.ID)
779					}
780					emit("pre_claim_intake.warn", map[string]any{
781						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
782						"outcome": string(PreClaimIntakeError),
783						"detail":  warning,
784					})
785				default:
786					// Terminal non-actionable outcome: park with needs_human label so
787					// ReadyExecution filters this bead until an operator reviews it.
788					warning := trimDiagnosticPrefix(intakeResult.Detail, "pre-claim intake")
789					if warning == "" {
790						warning = string(intakeOutcome)
791					}
792					if runtime.Log != nil {
793						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "pre-claim intake: %s (releasing %s)\n", warning, candidate.ID)
794					}
795					emit("pre_claim_intake.blocked", map[string]any{
796						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
797						"outcome": string(intakeOutcome),
798						"detail":  warning,
799					})
800					if berr := parkBeadPostIntakeRejection(w.Store, candidate.ID, assignee, intakeOutcome, intakeResult.Detail, now().UTC()); berr != nil && runtime.Log != nil {
801						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "pre-claim intake park error: %v\n", berr)
802					}
803					_ = w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID)
804					if runtime.Once {
805						applyStop(work.StopInput{Once: true})
806						return result, nil
807					}
808					continue
809				}
810			}
811	
812			if runtime.PreDispatchLintHook != nil {
813				lintResult, lintErr := runtime.PreDispatchLintHook(ctx, candidate.ID)
814				lintThreshold := rcfg.BeadQualityLintBlockThresholdScore()
815				appendPreDispatchLintEvent(w.Store, candidate.ID, lintResult, lintErr, lintThreshold, assignee, now().UTC())
816	
817				if lintErr != nil {
818					if runtime.Log != nil {
819						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "pre-dispatch lint: %v (continuing with %s)\n", lintErr, candidate.ID)
820					}
821					emit("pre_dispatch_lint.warn", map[string]any{
822						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
823						"warning": lintErr.Error(),
824					})
825				} else if lintThreshold > 0 && lintResult.Score < lintThreshold {
826					blockMsg := fmt.Sprintf(
827						"pre-dispatch lint blocked dispatch for %s: score=%d below threshold=%d; see bead-lifecycle MODE: lint guidance in .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md",

829					)
830					if runtime.Log != nil {
831						_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(runtime.Log, blockMsg)
832					}
833					emit("pre_dispatch_lint.blocked", map[string]any{
834						"bead_id":          candidate.ID,
835						"score":            lintResult.Score,
836						"threshold_score":  lintThreshold,
837						"skill":            "bead-lifecycle",
838						"skill_path":       ".agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md",
839						"dispatch_skipped": true,
840						"warning_mode":     false,
841						"rationale":        lintResult.Rationale,
842						"suggested_fixes":  lintResult.SuggestedFixes,
843						"waivers_applied":  lintResult.WaiversApplied,
844					})
845					_ = w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID)
846					if runtime.Once {
847						applyStop(work.StopInput{Once: true})
848						return result, nil
849					}
850					continue
851				}
852			}
853	
854			// Generate a provisional attempt_id for progress events.
855			// The real attempt_id is assigned inside ExecuteBead; we use this
856			// for queueing/running events and replace with the real one once known.
857			provAttemptID := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomProgressID()
858			runStart := now()
859			phaseSeq := 0
860			nextPhase := func(phase string, heartbeat bool) {
861				phaseSeq++
862				emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, newProgressEvent(
863					runtime.WorkerID, runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, provAttemptID,
864					harness, model, profile,
865					phase, phaseSeq, heartbeat, now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
866				))
867			}
868	
869			nextPhase("queueing", false)
870	
871			hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
872			var hbWG sync.WaitGroup
873			hbWG.Add(1)
874			go func(beadID string) {
875				defer hbWG.Done()
876				ticker := time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval)
877				defer ticker.Stop()
878				for {
879					select {
880					case <-hbCtx.Done():
881						return
882					case <-ticker.C:
883						_ = w.Store.TouchClaimHeartbeat(beadID)
884					}
885				}
886			}(candidate.ID)
887	
888			nextPhase("running", false)
889	
890			// tryExecutor preserves the legacy w.Executor.Execute(ctx, candidate.ID)
891			// invocation while letting try.Attempt own conflict recovery.
892			attemptOut, err := agenttry.Attempt(ctx, w.Store, candidate.ID, agenttry.AttemptOpts{
893				Bead:                candidate,
894				Executor:            tryExecutor(w.Executor),
895				Store:               w.Store,
896				ProjectRoot:         runtime.ProjectRoot,
897				SatisfactionChecker: w.SatisfactionChecker,
898				VerificationRunner:  w.VerificationRunner,
899				AutoRecover:         tryAutoRecover(w.conflictAutoRecoverFn),
900				ConflictResolver:    w.ConflictResolver,
901				Assignee:            assignee,
902				Now:                 now,
903				Cooldown:            LandConflictCooldown,
904			})
905			hbCancel()
906			hbWG.Wait()
907			report := fromTryReport(attemptOut.Report)
908			if report.BeadID == "" {
909				report.BeadID = candidate.ID
910			}
911			if report.Status == "" {
912				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
913			}
914			if report.Detail == "" {
915				report.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(report.Status, "", "")
916			}
917			classifyLoopReportFailure(&report)
918			if IsResourceExhaustedStatus(report.Status) {
919				result.Attempts++
920				exitReason = "resource_exhausted"
921				if err := w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID); err != nil {
922					_ = commitOutcome(ctx, w.Store, candidate.ID, func() error {
923						return commitOutcomeError("Unclaim", assignee, result, err)
924					})
925					if ctx.Err() != nil {
926						return result, ctx.Err()
927					}
928					return result, err
929				}
930				emitResourceExhausted(emit, w.Store, candidate.ID, report, assignee, now().UTC())
931				result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
932				result.Failures++
933				result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
934				if err := w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC())); err != nil {
935					_ = commitOutcome(ctx, w.Store, candidate.ID, func() error {
936						return commitOutcomeError("AppendEvent", assignee, result, err)
937					})
938					if ctx.Err() != nil {
939						return result, ctx.Err()
940					}
941					return result, err
942				}
943				finalAttemptID := report.AttemptID
944				if finalAttemptID == "" {
945					finalAttemptID = provAttemptID
946				}
947				phaseSeq++
948				emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
949					EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
950					AttemptID: finalAttemptID,
951					WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
952					ProjectID: runtime.ProjectRoot,
953					BeadID:    candidate.ID,
954					Harness:   harness,
955					Model:     model,
956					Profile:   profile,
957					Phase:     "failed",
958					PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
959					Heartbeat: false,
960					TS:        now().UTC(),
961					ElapsedMS: now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
962					Message:   report.Detail,
963				})
964				emit("bead.result", map[string]any{
965					"bead_id":              candidate.ID,
966					"status":               report.Status,
967					"detail":               report.Detail,
968					"session_id":           report.SessionID,
969					"result_rev":           report.ResultRev,
970					"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
971					"preserve_ref":         report.PreserveRef,
972					"no_changes_rationale": report.NoChangesRationale,
973					"duration_ms":          now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
974				})
975				if runtime.Log != nil {
976					_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(runtime.Log, ResourceExhaustedStopMessage)
977				}
978				return result, nil
979			}
980			// ddx-5b3e57f4: distinguish worker-disrupted from model-gave-up.
981			// A failed attempt where the loop ctx was cancelled, or the executor
982			// surfaced a transport-class error, is not evidence the model could
983			// not make progress — skip the no-progress cooldown so the bead is
984			// immediately re-claimable. If the executor itself already marked
985			// report.Disrupted (e.g. a future SIGKILL/SIGTERM or server-restart
986			// classifier inside ExecuteBeadWithConfig), preserve it.
987			if !report.Disrupted && report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
988				if reason, disrupted := classifyDisruption(ctx, err); disrupted {
989					report.Disrupted = true
990					report.DisruptionReason = reason
991				}
992			}
993			if report.Disrupted {
994				reason := report.DisruptionReason
995				if reason == "" {
996					reason = "unknown"
997				}
998				emitDisruptionDetected(emit, w.Store, candidate.ID,
999					reason, report.Detail, report.Harness, report.Model, assignee, now().UTC())
1000			}
1001	
1002			result.Attempts++
1003			appendExecutionRoutingIntentEvidence(w.Store, candidate, report, now().UTC())
1004	
1005			if attemptOut.StoreErr != nil {
1006				_ = commitOutcome(ctx, w.Store, candidate.ID, func() error {
1007					return commitOutcomeError(attemptOut.StoreErrOp, assignee, result, attemptOut.StoreErr)
1008				})
1009			}
1010	
1011			if parking := attemptOut.Parking; parking != nil {
1012				if parking.Unclaim {
1013					if err := w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID); err != nil {
1014						_ = commitOutcome(ctx, w.Store, candidate.ID, func() error {
1015							return commitOutcomeError("Unclaim", assignee, result, err)
1016						})
1017						if ctx.Err() != nil {
1018							return result, ctx.Err()
1019						}
1020						continue
1021					}
1022				}
1023				if parking.RunPostAttemptTriage {
1024					report = w.runPostAttemptTriage(ctx, candidate, report, runtime, assignee, now)
1025				}
1026				if parking.Event != nil {
1027					_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
1028						Kind:      parking.Event.Kind,
1029						Summary:   parking.Event.Summary,
1030						Body:      parking.Event.Body,
1031						Actor:     assignee,

1362	// emitPickerPrioritySkips fires a picker.priority_skip event when the picker
1363	// chose `chosen` while at least one strictly higher-priority bead was passed
1364	// over (ddx-9d55601f AC #4). Skips at the same priority as `chosen` are NOT
1365	// reported — those are FIFO order or label-filter siblings, not starvation.
1366	//
1367	// The emit is guarded so it costs nothing when the chosen bead is the highest-
1368	// priority bead the picker saw (the common case).
1369	func emitPickerPrioritySkips(emit func(string, map[string]any), chosen bead.Bead, skips []pickerSkip) {
1370		if len(skips) == 0 {
1371			return
1372		}
1373		var higher []map[string]any
1374		for _, s := range skips {
1375			if s.Priority < chosen.Priority {
1376				higher = append(higher, map[string]any{
1377					"bead_id":    s.BeadID,
1378					"priority":   s.Priority,
1379					"queue_rank": queueRankValue(s.BeadRank),
1380					"reason":     s.Reason,
1381				})
1382			}
1383		}
1384		if len(higher) == 0 {
1385			return
1386		}
1387		emit("picker.priority_skip", map[string]any{
1388			"chosen_bead_id":  chosen.ID,
1389			"chosen_priority": chosen.Priority,
1390			"skipped":         higher,
1391		})
1392	}
1393	
1394	// pickerSkip records a single bead that the picker passed over while looking
1395	// for the next candidate. It is used to emit a structured picker.priority_skip
1396	// event when a worker claims a lower-priority bead while a higher-priority
1397	// bead was skipped, so future starvation regressions are observable.
1398	//
1399	// Reason values are bounded to the set named in ddx-9d55601f AC #4:
1400	//
1401	//	label_filter, in_attempted, claim_race, eligibility_filter, retry_cooldown
1402	//
1403	// Note: eligibility_filter and retry_cooldown are applied upstream in
1404	// Store.ReadyExecution (so beads filtered for those reasons never reach
1405	// nextCandidate at all). They are part of the reason vocabulary so future
1406	// picker rearrangements can re-emit them without changing the schema.
1407	type pickerSkip struct {
1408		BeadID   string
1409		Priority int
1410		BeadRank *int
1411		Reason   string
1412	}
1413	
1414	func hasGuardSkips(skips []pickerSkip) bool {
1415		for _, skip := range skips {
1416			switch skip.Reason {
1417			case "label_filter", "in_attempted", "claim_race", "eligibility_filter", "retry_cooldown", "target_bead":
1418				continue
1419			default:
1420				return true
1421			}
1422		}
1423		return false
1424	}
1425	
1426	func queueRankValue(raw any) any {
1427		rank, ok := parseQueueRank(raw)
1428		if !ok {
1429			return nil
1430		}
1431		return rank
1432	}
1433	
1434	// nextCandidate returns the next claimable bead from the execution-ready
1435	// queue along with the list of higher-priority beads it skipped (and the
1436	// reason for each skip). The returned skips slice is only meaningful when
1437	// ok=true: it contains every entry that came BEFORE the chosen candidate
1438	// in the priority-sorted ReadyExecution result.
1439	//
1440	// It delegates filter+sort to PreviewQueue and then additionally filters out
1441	// beads already present in the current drain results slice (which is
1442	// non-deterministic across runs and therefore excluded from the stable
1443	// PreviewQueue surface).
1444	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) nextCandidate(ctx context.Context, results []ExecuteBeadReport, guards []work.Guard, labelFilter, targetBeadID string) (bead.Bead, []pickerSkip, bool, error) {
1445		// Use PreviewQueue for the stable filter+sort logic. Limit=0 returns all
1446		// entries so we can scan for the first candidate not already present in
1447		// the current drain results slice.
1448		entries, err := PreviewQueue(w.Store, PickerFilters{LabelFilter: labelFilter}, 0)
1449		if err != nil {
1450			return bead.Bead{}, nil, false, err
1451		}
1452	
1453		// Rebuild the ready list from the preview entries in picker order so we
1454		// can apply the per-run drain results slice on top. We need the original
1455		// bead values for the return; fetch them from ReadyExecution (already
1456		// ordered).
1457		ready, err := w.Store.ReadyExecution()
1458		if err != nil {
1459			return bead.Bead{}, nil, false, err
1460		}
1461		// Index by ID for O(1) lookup.
1462		byID := make(map[string]bead.Bead, len(ready))
1463		for _, b := range ready {
1464			byID[b.ID] = b
1465		}
1466		var skips []pickerSkip
1467		for _, entry := range entries {
1468			candidate, ok := byID[entry.BeadID]
1469			if !ok {
1470				// Should not happen; skip defensively.
1471				continue
1472			}
1473			if targetBeadID != "" && candidate.ID != targetBeadID {
1474				skips = append(skips, pickerSkip{BeadID: candidate.ID, Priority: candidate.Priority, BeadRank: queueRankPtr(candidate.Extra["queue-rank"]), Reason: "target_bead"})
1475				continue
1476			}
1477			if hasResultForBead(results, candidate.ID) {
1478				skips = append(skips, pickerSkip{BeadID: candidate.ID, Priority: candidate.Priority, BeadRank: queueRankPtr(candidate.Extra["queue-rank"]), Reason: "in_attempted"})
1479				continue
1480			}
1481			if entry.FilterDecision == FilterDecisionSkipped {
1482				// PreviewQueue already applied label_filter; record as skip.
1483				skips = append(skips, pickerSkip{BeadID: candidate.ID, Priority: candidate.Priority, BeadRank: queueRankPtr(candidate.Extra["queue-rank"]), Reason: "label_filter"})
1484				continue
1485			}
1486			allowed := true
1487			for _, guard := range guards {
1488				if guard == nil {
1489					continue
1490				}
1491				ok, reason := guard.Allow(ctx, candidate.ID)
1492				if ok {
1493					continue
1494				}
1495				allowed = false
1496				if reason != "" {
1497					skips = append(skips, pickerSkip{BeadID: candidate.ID, Priority: candidate.Priority, BeadRank: queueRankPtr(candidate.Extra["queue-rank"]), Reason: reason})
1498				}
1499				break
1500			}
1501			if !allowed {
1502				continue
1503			}
1504			return candidate, skips, true, nil
1505		}
1506		return bead.Bead{}, skips, false, nil
1507	}

1512				return true
1513			}
1514		}
1515		return false
1516	}
